prompt: DR2 (imputation quality) was compared between two imputation runs of the same 480 GBS samples using different reference panels. Interpret the differences and recommend which panel to use.
context:
DR2 comparison by MAF bin:

  MAF_bin       Panel_A (1,354 indica)   Panel_B (3,010 all subpops)   Diff (A-B)
  0.01-0.05     0.72                     0.68                          +0.04
  0.05-0.10     0.84                     0.82                          +0.02
  0.10-0.20     0.91                     0.90                          +0.01
  0.20-0.50     0.95                     0.95                           0.00

All 480 GBS samples are indica.
Panel A: 1,354 indica WGS samples
Panel B: 3,010 WGS samples (all 5 subpopulations)

Compute time per chromosome:
  Panel A: 18 minutes
  Panel B: 52 minutes
correct_answer: Panel A (indica-only) provides slightly better imputation quality for rare variants (DR2 +0.04 for MAF 0.01-0.05) while being 2.9x faster. The advantage is concentrated at low MAF because rare indica-specific variants have more informative haplotype templates in a matched panel -- the non-indica samples in Panel B contribute haplotypes that are rarely selected by the HMM for indica targets, diluting the effective reference. For common variants (MAF>0.20), the panels perform identically (DR2=0.95) because these variants are well-imputed regardless of reference composition. Recommendation: use Panel A (indica-only) for this dataset. The benefits are: (1) better rare variant accuracy, (2) 2.9x faster computation, (3) lower memory usage. The only scenario where Panel B might be preferred is if some GBS samples have indica-aus or indica-japonica admixed ancestry, where non-indica haplotypes would provide better templates for the admixed segments.
rubric: Full marks (3): Must note Panel A is better for rare variants, explain why (haplotype matching), recommend Panel A with computational advantage, and caveat about admixed samples. Mostly correct (2): Recommends A correctly with reasoning but misses admixture caveat. Partial (1): Notes Panel A is slightly better but weak reasoning. Incorrect (0): Recommends Panel B because bigger is always better.
notes: Directly validates the reasoning tested in tier4_method_009 but at T2 level -- interpreting empirical results rather than making a prospective decision.
